preamble: A direct shift from [ʌ] to [r] is rare because [ʌ] is a short vowel that does not typically occur at word boundaries where 'intrusive R' is found. However, in dialects where the STRUT vowel merges with the NURSE vowel ([ɜːr]), or when [ʌ] reduces to a schwa, an [r] can be inserted.
phoneticEffects: Vowel Reduction, Intrusive R, Nurse-Strut Merger.

examples.note: When the short vowel reduces to a schwa, an intrusive R can be added to prevent hiatus before the next vowel.
